posts.author: Belal Sucks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Burns vs. Malott
Mike Malott, TKO, R1
So stupid that this is a main event but being in Canada on a fight night it atleast kind of makees sense. Still, its a pretty interesting fight on paper; Burns has had a great career and looked super high level and versatile at times but age has seemingly caught up to him horribly. These younger, bigger, and hungrier guys keep making quick work of him and honestly its kind of sad. I would much rather see him fight some other Unc's from his era but when you hold a ranking in the UFC for a long time this tends to be how they ship you off. Im not even huge on Malott but he is powerful and aggressive early on in fights so I think he can clip Gilbert rounds 1 or 2 and make easy work of him.

Jasudavicius vs. Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Going Jasmine by decision all day here. She got embarassed by Fiorot her last time out but honestly she was never at that level so its not surprising. I think Silva though is even a step below that despite the high finish rate and Fighting Nerds plot armor. I see Jasmine using her physicality to get takedowns and win a lopsided decision.

Castañeda vs. Vologdin
John Castañeda, DEC
Very rarely can you say this but Vologdin is way too small for Batamweight. He had an amazing fight on contender serries but lets not forget that the other dude made his face look like a ketchup sandwich. The toughness is there and the striking is solid but he's not gonna KO anyone unless he drops to 125lbs. I think that size and strength disadvantage will be evident here as Castaneda is going to look to implement his wrestling heavy style. I think this goes to decision but dont be shocked if we see Sexi Mexi hold him down and put him away with ground and pound.

Choi vs. Lima
André Lima, DEC
I know nothing about Choi but I know for a fact that Lima will be a ranked flyweight by the end of 2026. If you've never seen this guy fight you better watch and take notice because he is the future.

Boser vs. Saricam
Tanner Boser, TKO, R1
Boser back at heavyweight now? Lowkey kind of sad to see it. Either way, I think I trust him more than random 35 year old Turkish heavyweight prospect. Pretty sure someone is getting KO'd early in this fight regardless so Im gonna side with the guy that I know is a tough out.

posts.author: Goat******76
posts.content:
[Predictions]

The amount of people putting the house on Vologdin instead of Castaneda has me thinking the microplastics corporations have been dumping in the ocean has finally started reducing IQ rates. For one, Vologdin is entering off of a loss on the contender series. Good fight, don’t get me wrong, the kid is clearly a savage and has grit and balls, but getting a contract off a loss on the contender series is pretty much a guarantee your career isn’t gonna go anywhere (see Jose Medina and Angel Pacheco) Secondly, Vologdin’s resume is absolutely poultry in comparison to Castaneda. John has more than three times the experience, has much better wins (Miles Johns, Gafurov, Kyung) and even in the fights he’s lost they were against strong competition and was able to fair well against them (nearly gave Daniel Santos a 10 - 8 in the first round). Not only do I think John wins this, I think he’s mops the ******* floor with Mark, rings him like a wet mop.

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Burns vs. Malott
Gilbert Burns, DEC
Gilbert Burns main event in the big 2026. I actually think he wins this though lol. On one hand his losses to Brady and Morales have aged well for him. I thought his career was over whenever he fought Morales, it looked like he just went out there and just lost before it started. But then Morales did that to Brady too. Turns out, something about Morales' size, power, and style of striking mathces up with the short stocky grappler type welterweights to have these weird performances where he pitches an absolute shutout. So if you toss out that fight, Burns went 5 hard rounds with Brady, fought a war with JDM, and the Belal fight was just a weird one because of injuries to both of the two. Then look at Mallot, dude had to cheat to beat Kevin Holland, lost to Neil Magny, decision with Trevin Giles. He is not good, Burns was good at one point, is definitely slipping, but I trust someone who was good at one point and maybe isnt anymore to beat someone who was never good in the first place.

Phillips vs. Jourdain
Charles Jourdain, DEC
This is a banger, but also shouldn't be a comain event. Phillips is gonna look like an absolute world beater in round 1, hopping around, landing big shots, throwing dynamic ****, looks like the second coming of Anderson Silva with a bit of Wonderboy mixed in. Then round 2 will come, and Phillips will suddenly look like CM Punk, and Jourdain will destroy him on the feet, take him down at will, kick the legs constantly. Definitely a chance Jourdain gets the sub, but I'm thinking its a decision, Kyler doesn't really get finished.

Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Don't care.

We have a dude on a two fight losing streak fighting a dude who lost on the contender series. Give me a ******* break. I don't care, Castaneda sucks.

Burns vs. Malott

I miss prime Gilbert. He was actually so exciting and was an elite level welterweight. And now he is just so SLOWWWWWWWWWWWWW. He’s always been rather chinny, but man his chin has really declined as well and he has terrible reactions everytime he gets hit now. He’s still a very high level grappler when it comes to skill, but man his grappling has honestly declined as well both offensively and defensively. Sean Brady is an elite level grappler, but the way Gilbert just accepted getting thrown around for 5 rounds was really alarming. And offensively, we saw against Morales he really couldn’t do anything. He actually did land one takedown, but couldn’t do a damn thing with it. And then got pieced up and KO’d seconds later. I can honestly see this fight going the exact same way. Malott is really not that good and the fade is definitely coming soon, but for what it’s worth his striking and grappling are both decent. His grappling is decent enough to nullify this version of Gilbert’s attempts to grapple, and his striking is decent enough to hurt Gilbert really badly here I think. He also hits pretty damn hard. I don’t see this fight leaving round 2. I really don’t.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Croden vs Zheleznyakova
Zheleznyakova DEC

This should actually be a decent WMMA fight I think. Both of these girls are strikers who go for it; I don’t think we see a finish but these two are gonna throw down. This is a tricky one to call, I think Croden should be the favorite but I’ve always had a difficult time rating her. She looked solid in her debut but was also getting hit a lot. Lisboa also death gassed and kind of gave up so that was weird. Croden is a good striker and really physical for the weight class, but I kind of think this is a bad matchup for her. One of the best things about Croden is that she’s huge for the weight class; she doesn’t have the greatest technical ability but she’s bigger than everyone she fights and thusly can bully them. In this fight, she’s not only missing the size advantage, she’s fighting someone with the exact same measurements. I think this hurts Croden because she won’t be able to get away with some of her usual bad habits. Dariya is a bad striker but for WMMA she is effective; she is aggressive and throws hard. She mostly throws the same combo over and over but it works because she’s decently athletic and hits hard. Neither girl wears damage well at all, but I think Croden’s defense is legitimately bad. She is very stiff and gets hit a lot, I think this could be a problem against someone aggressive and the same size as her. Dariya’s face gets cut so easily due to her botox which is a problem, but she can just as easily cut Croden here. If Croden wrestles she is going to have a ton of success; Dariya has decent first layer tdd but once you get her down the round is over. She has nothing to offer on her back and has been embarrassed on the mat in both of her losses. She got the Mullins loss back but even in the rematch she still got taken down too easily. Watching the tape back though, Croden can offensively wrestle but she doesn’t do so very often. She did it in her debut but that was only after Lisboa death gassed and Croden realized she wanted out. She could wrestle Dariya here but I don’t think Croden’s takedowns are that good, nor is her control really. I just worry since Dariya has a nonexistent ground game. In a mostly striking affair, I like Dariya’s chances here. Her technique isn’t great but for WMMA standards I think she’s pretty solid. She’s aggressive and powerful as well as having the size to match Croden. I think it will be competitive since both girls get hit a lot, but I like Dariya here as the younger, faster girl with opportunities to land bigger.

Aldrich vs Horth
Horth DEC

I did not tape this one. Not only because I don’t care but there’s also way too many decisions on the Aldrich side for me to bother doing a deep dive. This line does seem wide since Horth is old and fights close with everyone, but so does Aldrich. She’s also 34 so it’s not like there’s a wide age gap here. Horth has shown some really bad looks on the ground in her losses/Ivana fight, but Aldrich doesn’t wrestle and will just cage push her at worst, so I don’t think that should matter much. Even if she does try, Aldrich is a bad wrestler and Horth has historically always been hard to take down. Horth also has a pretty big physicality advantage which goes a long way in WMMA; especially here, as Aldrich has always struggled against more physical girls. The striking will probably be close but Horth should win. She has solid kickboxing and fights at range pretty well. I like her kicks and she has looked really good on the feet in her last two. She’s fought two cans but she ko’ed Bleda and was hurting Vanessa in every exchange. I remember her buzzing Horth as well, so she’s got some power for 125. That should probably go a long way here since Aldrich can’t hurt anyone and is very hittable herself. I expect this to be a really boring fight. I also wouldn’t be shocked if Horth wins a robbery either. We always see bad hometown judging in the UFC but Canada has some historically bad scorecards, and Horth has been in some ugly, close fights before.

Raposo vs Nascimento
Nascimento SUB RD 2

In what seems to be the theme of this entire card, we’ve got a pretty bizarre matchup here. Nascimento should be fighting in the top 15 but his inactivity and weight misses have prevented him, and Raposo is coming off of a big upset where he saved his job. Raposo is a weird fighter because I don’t think he’s that good, but he’s also durable and well rounded everywhere. He’s looked bad in the ufc and I wasn’t that impressed with his most recent win either, but tbf he’s also had a bunch of really tough fights. He’s a nothingburger but also annoying because he’s durable and good enough everywhere to put up a solid fight. Nascimento has been my guy for a while but age and inactivity is starting to catch up with him; there’s been some signs of regression in his last two, and he’s also struggling to make 125 as well. Nascimento has elite bjj but really bad wrestling. He doesn’t offensively wrestle that much and can be taken down by a gust of wind. His striking is solid but in his last fight he was looking really bad until he wasn’t; considering how Durden looked in his last fight, that performance is not aging well. Durden was pressuring him and walking him down, causing Nascimento to panic. He was able to rock Durden and find the sub once he shot a panic td, but he was looking pretty bad up until that point. His prior fight against Filho is a good win, but he looked pretty bad there as well. This is obviously a much different matchup compared to Raposo, but Filho was honestly dominating Nascimento on top and lost the fight making some really bad mistakes. Raposo is a big step down from these guys but I do take some pause seeing Nascimento as a big favorite since the weight cut takes a lot out of him and he is showing signs of regression. That being said, I think he is the better fighter here. His striking hasn’t looked great lately but he does have a big size advantage here; Raposo is small even for 125 while Nascimento should probably be at 135. Raposo is a decent boxer but the problem is he does nothing out there. He has good fundamentals and some solid power but he isn’t aggressive and is always just a step behind his opponents. He was able to outstrike Maksum last fight, but Maksum only wanted to wrestle. Maksum is a fraud as well, so I don’t think that win is as impressive as it looks. I think Nascimento should be fine on the feet but I do worry if this becomes a majority striking match. He can win but it is going to be a close fight since Nascimento is low volume himself and not comfortable getting hit. He will offensively wrestle but I don’t think his takedowns are that good. Nascimento has also been matched mostly with wrestlers/grapplers, so he doesn’t need to wrestle the majority of the time. Raposo can be taken down, and the size advantage should help Nascimento with wrestling, but I do worry a little bit since Raposo’s got decent first layer takedown defense. However, if Nascimento can get the body lock or even get Raposo to offensively wrestle, he should dominate. Nascimento’s bjj is very high level and while he has some very bad habits on bottom, he’s also got a great guard and an ability to find reversals in key moments. We’ve seen him outgrappled much better wrestlers/grapplers than Raposo, and he even had an argument to beat Tagir. I do worry a little bit about his ability to win minutes but Nascimento should cruise once he gets this to the ground; he can even take the back standing and find a submission that way. I wouldn’t be shocked if that’s the go-to here given that Raposo is very tiny. Raposo is a black belt but his grappling is meh; I always thought it was a really bad look how Hadley dominated him the second he got on top. Raposo won r1 and then Hadley got to the back in r2 and quickly ended the fight. Nascimento is showing signs of slowing down but I think he is still a level above Raposo. I’m picking sub since I think Nasicmento will find a way to get it to the ground and is the far superior grappler, but I wouldn’t be shocked if Raposo fights very passive and forces him into a low volume sparring match like all his ufc fights have been. The over/fight goes to DEC could be sneaky here.

Castaneda vs Vologdin
Vologdin KO RD 3

This is incredible matchmaking; I would not be shocked if this ends up being FOTN. Vologdin is coming off of one of the best fights of all time in DWCS, and Castaneda is someone whose always in high paced scraps. Stylistically this makes a lot of sense but for a debut this is honestly a tricky fight for Vologdin. I like his skillset but I hate him at 135; he is built like a little kid and is at a huge size disparity here. Castaneda is also insanely experienced and pretty much exclusively competed against guys in the top 20-25 his entire career, so it’s not like he’s getting a layup. Castaneda can not only fight the exact type of fight Vologdin wants, he’s got grappling that he can go to as well. Seeing Castaneda as an underdog kind of seems like a no brainer here, but the more I looked into it, I kind of think this may be a tricky fight for him. When he’s allowed to have his way, Castaneda is a problem. He can do a bit of everything and forces a hellacious pace in every fight. His achilles heel is that he is a great hammer, and an awful nail. When he gets put on the backfoot his entire game falls apart and he turns into a punching bag. The best example of this is his fight with Willycat; Castaneda genuinely looked elite for 5 minutes, then once Willycat survived and got him going backwards, he quickly gassed out and got finished. His losses against Marcos/Gutierrez went similarly as well; the latter being extremely frustrating as he had a huge advantage with grappling yet didn’t bother to wrestle until he was clearly down two rounds. Even in his wins Castaneda struggles when you get him going backwards: Johns for example was beating him to the punch for most of the fight unil he gassed, and he had to resort to wrestling Muin to win because he couldn’t deal with the constant forward pressure. Vologdin is a bad nail himself, but in the Martinetti fight he showed that he is a complete terminator; he may not like to be the nail but he is just going to tank everything until he can’t and keep coming forward. The same cannot be said for Castaneda, if you put him on the backfoot he falls apart quickly and becomes super hittable. Whoever takes the first step here wins this fight, and I think Vologdin can be the nail. The pressure he showed on DWCS was insane, and I don’t think Castaneda will like that. He’s a really solid striker and can match Vologdin’s output, but he will not like the pressure and power of Vologdin. Castaneda doesn’t really gas but Vologdin is going to constantly swing to the body, which could pay dividends especially if Castaneda is getting backed up. Castaneda will be able to get Vologdin going backwards at times but I think he will be able to stop the pressure. Castaneda doesn’t have much power and is a death-by-thousand-cuts type of striker, but we know Vologdin is durable as hell and will march through it; unless Castaneda can drop him bad, I think Vologdin will be able to stop the pressure by landing big shots of his own. Once he gets Castaneda going backwards I think Vologdin should be able to control the fight. He did slow a little bit in r3 on dwcs but the pace of that fight was just ridiculous; what he showed in the first 10 minutes should be enough to hang with Castaneda; without his opponents gassing I don’t think his style is nearly as effective. The one thing that worries me here is takedowns. Vologdin weirdly has solid first layer tdd and good grappling, but he has shown soooo many bad looks off of his back. Most were earlier in his career but still, in his lone losses he got rinsed on the mat. He would show good tdd and do the right things on the ground, but once reversed/taken down he got mauled. He did better on dwcs but once tired he did concede tds and let Martinetti control him; he didn’t get in any trouble but I would like to see some improvements. Castaneda is not an amazing wrestler but he will mix it in. That being said, Vologdin can stop the td threat with pressure; Castaneda just lost what should've been a very easy matchup against Gutierrez because he made Castaneda back up the entire fight, stopping him from shooting. The last point is you always have to deal with the question of can Castaneda make it to the cage? He has had a slew of health issues his entire career, and come fight day he usually has a lot of trouble making weight. He always needs a CW and is inactive on ig, so I can only assume this layoff was due to injuries/health issues. He’s 35 now and hasn’t fought in a while, so I do wonder if he’s kind of washed. I don’t feel great about backing Vologdin here since this is a pretty tough debut, but stylistically I think he can have some success here. Castaneda has also been looking pretty washed as of late so this may be an opportune time to fight him. Either way this fight is going to be a war.

Siraj vs Yannis
Siraj SUB RD 1

Such a random last minute addition to this card. I do enjoy this new trend of signing local talent for cards but could they at least sign good fighters? Siraj is really fun to watch but also a very bizarre fighter. He is a slow starter who goes life and death with everyone, yet has insane cardio, really high level scrambling ability, and a ton of experience. In r1 he might be the worst fighter of all time; he drops the first round against everyone and usually gets dominated, against low level comp as well. He will get dropped, taken down with no resistance, put in submissions, etc. His scrambling game is very high level imo but he also gets taken down with no effort and can be put in awful positions. That being said, he is able to find his way out and get on top/to the back. He chains subs together and stays active with gnp. It’s a really bad look seeing him go life and death with everyone, and I’m not sure how far he will go with that style in the UFC. That being said, Yannis is really bad and one of the worst grapplers Siraj has fought in a while. Yannis has some wrestling but is predominantly a striker. Most of the people Siraj is fighting look to shoot on him, but I don’t think Yannis will do that. Siraj’s offensive wrestling isn’t anything special but he should be able to get Yannis down; Yannis has a basic understanding of grappling/wrestling but he’s very limited. He gets taken down by everyone and is a very bad grappler; he doesn’t know what to do when his back gets taken and is pretty clueless on the mat. Siraj gets taken down/reversed/almost subbed against most guys but I’m not sure if Yannis can threaten much here, and I also think Siraj’s scrambles are a level above what Yannis is used to. Siraj is a weasel on the mat and is very good at taking the back or ending up on top. I think he should be able to find something and overwhelm Yannis. Siraj showed some awful looks on the mat before so I am kind of worried in his ability to get it done here, but at the same time Yannis has no grappling and is pretty much a pure striker. Yannis is definitely a better striker here and while early I think he could drop Siraj, but his striking is very basic. He is a low volume boxer with some decent power, but not very skilled overall. Siraj has awful defense and gets dropped all the time, but much like with his grappling he forces a crazy pace. He is not the most technical but he will stay in your face and doesn’t **** off; against low level guys like Yannis I think he could have success here. I want to take r2 since SIraj is a slow starter and has shown some really bad looks, but I also think this matchup presents opportunities for him to get it done early. Yannis is a striker with bad bjj, and is very one dimensional compared to who Siraj has been fighting prior. Siraj is also coming down to 135 for the first time since 2019, so he will have a size advantage here. I also expect him to get manhandled less at 135, since he should be more physical/on par with the rest of the guys in the division. I expect this to be a classic Siraj fight where he loses r1 pretty badly and gets a comeback, but I also think Yannis is super low level and limited, so this may be easier than it seems for Siraj. Either way I expect him to win, Yannis is your typical Fury FC jobber.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Jasudavicius vs Silva
Jasudavicius DEC

This should be an easy fight to get Jasmine back in the W column. Losing to Manon is nothing shameful but watching her get smoked like that was pretty crazy. Jasmine has never had good striking defense but I didn’t expect her to get KO’ed so badly. I’m definitely a little worried given she’s 37 and lost all her momentum, but this should be a favorable matchup. I’ve never liked Karine since DWCS, and she has awful grappling habits that Jasmine can expose. Against unranked comp Karine was looking really scary, but once she started fighting ranked quality girls/girls who can match her strength, she looked very pedestrian. Silva is crazy strong and would use that to bully girls less skilled than her, but that won’t work here since Jasmine is stronger and a much better athlete; her athleticism is one of the main reasons she ended up being as good as she has been. I do favor Karine if this stays on the feet but she is very low volume and even more hittable than Jasmine. Karine has a ton of power which could be an issue since Jasmine is very sloppy and doesn’t have a great chin anyways (been rocked a lot actually), but as she has to do is avoid that sloppy overhand. I don’t think we will see much striking in this fight though. Jasmine is a very good wrestler as it is, and Karine is a guard player with no tdd and awful habits. She was losing handedly on dwcs to Yan, who was repeatedly raking her down with no resistance until she got meme subbed. Silva continued to win but these bad habits have continued. She’s been taken down and surprisingly uncontrolled in her las three fights. She does have an active guard but imo she really is not that dangerous outside of her initial sub attempts. I don’t see Jasmine putting her neck into a guillotine although I suppose it is possible. If she survives the front choke or triangle/armbar of Karine, I think Jasmine will get easy control here. Silva shows no resistance to the td and loves to play guard. This is a nightmare strategy against Jasmine, whose not only a great wrestler but her top time is insanely suppressive. She dominates pretty much everyone once on top and she does not lay n pray either. She will throw gnp and has multiple r3 finishes on her record. Silva may be able to stop punishment since she is a bjj nerd, but she is absolutely ****** off of her back here. She is going to need a hail mary sub to win because she lacks the skills to get to her feet and is at a huge wrestling disadvantage here. Silva is also a gasser as well; she slows down hard every fight and even more so when she’s the nail. I could lowkey see a finish here but I will be smart and pick Jasmine decision. She will get a lot of control time though, so a finish is always possible. If Karine somehow wins this fight, I think it will have to be a meme r1 KO/SUB, so maybe FDGTD could be a very sneaky play. This probably goes the distance at a high clip though, I just don’t like Karine.

Moises vs Young
Young KO RD 2

This card is packed with matchups that don’t make sense but this is definitely one of the weirdest ones by far. Moises usually fights vets or prospects, I guess Young is a prospect but not really one I would think to give the Moises test. This is a weird one in general, I saw Young was the slight favorite to open and thought that was odd, but after more tape it honestly makes sense. I’ve never been blown away by Young but he is a solid fighter. His record is ugly but he’s taken a lot of hard fights and has some good talent. His game is basic but he’s durable and fights at a high pace. Pre-tape I was doubting if he had the skills to outwork Moises on the feet, but watching his fights back, Moises is a really bad striker. It was always a little overrated to begin with but he has badly regressed on the feet in recent fights. Moises has good Muay Thai but all he does these days is spam leg kicks. His leg kicks are good but he rarely throws punches and is scared to engage these days. He just sits back at range throwing leg kicks and eating shots. He was always a counter striker even in his prime but he looks terrified to strike with everyone. Young’s striking is basic and I really don’t like his defense, but I think he can outwork Moises on the feet here. Moises will be able to land the leg kick which could be a problem, but I think Young has teh ability to outbox him. He mostly just throws a jab and 1-2 but he is aggressive and keeps a high pace. Moises has always struggled with guys who put him on the backfoot and pressure, and in current form I think he has a really bad time dealing with that. He can land on Young but I don’t think he has the power ot hurt him; he will need to chop at the legs. Young is front leg heavy and gets kind of plodding, so this could pay dividends. That being said, it’s hard to land leg kicks when you’re going backwards the whole time, and I think Moises will need to work the legs early or else he is going to have a hard time keeping up. He looks really scared to engage and getting ko’ed last fight was such a bad look; Gordon is a great vet but he doesn’t KO anyone. Moises didn’t have a second of success in that fight, got beat in every avenue of MMA, and then got slept cold. Again, Moises has always had his flaws of low output, bad reactions, bad tdd, but he looked completely out of sorts in that fight. His prior fight against Ogden was bad as well; I backed Moises confidently and I feel he got kind of lucky there. Ogden was easily winning on the feet and probably would’ve won r3 but ended up tearing his bicep when he took the back. Moises just looks disinterested on the feet and I think Young’s patented aggression will be a problem for him. Young also has really good tdd and growing up in Missouri, he has been wrestling for a long time. I don’t think he’s an elite anti-wrestler or anything but he has really solid tdd, great hips, and has dealt with chain wrestling a few times. He doesn’t panic when he does get taken down and showed really good defensive grappling against Salkilld as well. Moises is a good black belt but recently his grappling success has only came against short notice replacements or guys with no tdd/bottom game at all. If he takes teh back of Young it could be interesting, but I see Moises having a really difficult time getting takedowns here. For one, he isn’t aggressive enough with his attempts as it is, and he’s not a great chain wrestler either. I think Young will see the attempts coming at distance, and even if Moises is able to get to teh clinch/bodylock I think Young will be able to get up. He’s really hard to hold down and imo has faced better wrestlers than Moises before. Moises has really bad grappling habits of his own, if Young really wants he could score tds here and Moises will play bjj off of his back. This is a weird fight because even a washed Moises is a step up in comp, and despite winning his last fight I’m not really sure if Young is that good. Stylistically speaking though, I think he can cause a lot of issues for Moises. Moises has insane SOS but he has always been a very flawed fighter and lately has been looking awful. I think he is checked out and doesn’t have much left in him at this point.

Buzujka vs Barbosa
Barbosa KO RD 1

Banger to kick off the main card; I’ve been patiently awaiting the debut of Ticoto and this is a good opponent. Dennis is a jack of all trades, master of none; he can do a bit of everything but isn’t overly skilled or dangerous in any one area. He is a workhorse who grinded his way into the ufc but ultimately, I don’t think he belongs here. He’s not a can but he’s just not good enough in any one area to do much. Ticoto is a really fun fighter but I’m not sure what to think of him; I think he will win a lot of fights and probably even pull off a couple upsets, but the flaws in his game are really clear. Ticoto is a r1 KO or bust guy; his hardware is insane and he’s one of the hardest hitters at 145, but his cardio doesn’t support his style. Ticoto goes balls to the wall in r1 trying to kill you. His cardio isn’t that bad but he has a bad habit of going ape**** once he has you hurt. He nearly killed Santos which was impressive, but he was also completely spent after 3 minutes of brawling and died very shortly after. Ticoto is actually pretty measured, only going crazy once he finds a home for his bombs. However with Dennis’s style, I think he’s gonna come out guns blazing and not pacing himself. Dennis isn’t some cardio freak or anything but he is scrappy and will get into a brawl with Ticoto. I doubt this leaves r1 but if it does Ticoto might be in some trouble. He can win past r1 but he has to be the one controlling the pace; this should be possible against Dennis but he can take a shot and keep coming forward, which will cause Ticoto to keep a high pace the whole fight. Dennis doesn’t have much power at all so I think Ticoto should be fine if it gets extended, but you never know. I know they were a long time ago but the Felipe/Junior fights were pretty terrible looks imo. He couldn’t put the guys away and slowed down really badly, neither were a crazy high pace either. I do think Ticoto should find the r1 KO here though, he may be a glass cannon but his striking is legit and he is extremely dangerous. Dennis is scrappy but his technique is poor, he has no power, and he’s very hittable. He is tough but not that durable imo; he got bonked inside a minute against Emmers, got floored in r2 against Marshall, and was not reacting to shots all that well against Woodson. He’s too hittable and will fight the exact fight Ticoto wants, so I expect him to find the chin in r1. This should be a war for as long as it lasts.

Valentin vs Leblanc
Valentin SUB RD 2

Yet another completely random regional level fight on here. Leblanc was originally gonna be signed when this was in Manitoba as he’s from there and a surprisingly big draw for the Canadians. The card got moved but they decided to keep him. I understand why he’s the favorite here as Valentin really shouldn’t be getting another fight and has looked abysmal, but honestly I think this is one of the few guys he can beat. Valentin has looked atrocious in the ufc and his regional tape was not much better, but this is also a big step down in comp. Valentin fought two awful style matchups and then a mismatch in Ateba, now he gets to fight a 34 year old regional guy making his debut. This is a good matchup not just because of the intangibles, but Valentin has a big advantage on the mat here. Now, Valentin’s tdd is some of the worst ever, and even when he gets tds of his own he often times finds himself reversed. This is a possibility here as well, but I think he is a much better grappler than Leblanc. Valentin’s style doesn’t work in the ufc because his tdd is so bad, but his scrambling is pretty good imo. He is annoying af on the mat and stay active from all positions. Valentin also has good cardio and the way he got a lot of his wins was via causing chaos on the mat and tiring them out. His offensive takedowns are not good but Valentin has some solid judo he can use here. Leblanc forces the clinch in every fight so I think Valentin can have success with those tosses. Leblanc has really bad tdd and no real ground game either. His two losses he got dominated on the mat for basically 30 minutes and was really lost on what to do. I guess he has marginally improved in his time away, but he also hasn’t fought anyone good. The best guy he’s fought on this run was Weeks, and Weeks won 14:30 of that fight with no sweat. He was easily outwrestling Leblanc, cage pushing, and winning the striking but got dropped with a clinch knee to close r3 and they had to stop it. Again, Valentin is a really bad wrestler but I think he actually has the advantage here. Leblanc can get tds if he wants but he doesn’t have the top control or grappling to shut Valentin down; part of the reason he’s looked so bad in the ufc is because he’s been facing wrestlers with really good top control. Leblanc hasn’t shown me much of anything on the mat and I really think he could get swamped here if Valentin ends up on top. I think he will have much more opportunities to end up on top and find subs here than in prior fights. The striking will be ugly, I favor Leblanc slightly but I think Valentin can make it competitive. Leblanc’s technique is really bad, he just kind of flails his arms around aimlessly. He has some okay power but nothing special. The same goes for Valentin except he’s got an awful chin. He gets dropped in almost every fight and has drawn some really bad reactions throughout his career. I doubt Leblanc can ko him but he could score a KD or something. That being said, Leblanc is low volume and hittable, so I think the striking is pretty even. Like I said earlier, he loves to force the clinch as well, which is only going to benefit Valentin. This is gonna be super low level but I think this is a good fight for Valentin to save his job. He’s really bad but has some actual skills, Leblanc is a regional guy who is lucky to be here. Valentin is the younger, more skilled, and more experienced guy here. I know it’s in Canada but I actually think they gave Valentin this fight to see if they can get him to stay around. I think the UFC likes him, he’s just had an awful UFC run.

Boser vs Saricam
Saricam DEC

Continuing the theme of extremely random, nonsensical matchmaking, we’ve got this monstrosity on deck. I was wondering why they bothered to sign either guy, but then I found out this is an Ali Abdelaziz double dip. Boser is back after 1 fight in the last 3 years and Saricam had a run in Bellator. Saricam is fine and in terms of the division I’d say one of the better HW out there, but at the same time I wouldn’t say he’s anything special. Saricam is a very tough watch, he is a point fighter at HW and the definition of a nothing burger. He just kind of exists in the cage and wins by doing slightly more than his opponents. Boser does as well, so this fight is gonna be awful. Besides his manager and this being in Canada, I really don’t understand why Boser is getting another shot. He looked awful in his lone appearance outside the UFC and is back at HW, where he’s really bad. Boser’s whole gimmick at HW is that he’s just an LHW who doesn’t cut weight. He is a karate based striker and has some decently fast hands for HW, but that’s where the good qualities end. He has some decent power but in terms of HW’s he’s relatively weak; he hits just hard enough to stun you but doesn’t really possess a ton of power. Boser hasn’t added anything new in his time away, I’d actually argue he looked worse than normal against Moreira; Moreira is a pandemic era fighter who was one of the worst guys on the roster at the time, and Boser only beat him because he gassed out and quit. Boser lost r1 via wrestling and then just slowly grinded Moreira out as he went into cardiac arrest. The striking is going to be weird here. Boser definitely has more power (marginally) and a speed advantage but he’s a terrible minute winner. Boser explodes in bursts but outside of that he does a lot of nothing. Saricam is a point fighter at HW. He has almost no power and spends the entire fight circling the outside throwing the occasional jab. I wanted to favor him by a decent amount on the feet but I’m really worried he falls behind. It’s not like Boser is hard to outstrike but he at least has power and some speed. Saricam is pretty quick for a big guy as well, so maybe they will counter out. The striking is really gonna be awful here, these guys are going to have a low volume sparring match at range. I honestly want to favor Boser on the feet here, he’s awful but he at least does something on the feet; Saricam wins the striking on the thinnest of margins and doesn’t have much power either. He is ever so slightly more crafty than Boser though, and Boser becomes very ineffective once his opponents realize he hits like a *****. Saricam will wrestle here, and I think it will make all the difference. His wrestling is really mid and he does nothing on top, but at least it’s in his back pocket. Saricam has lost both his fights via being outwrestled and robbed Sowma pretty badly from being wrestled as well, so it’s a clear hole in his game. However, the same can be said for Boser. He has no tdd at all and lost multiple ufc fights he could’ve won (Latifi, Nascimento) because of his godawful tdd. He lost r1 clear as day to fat Moriera via wrestling, so this is not something he’s fixed either. I think Saricam will know to wrestle here, and he has gone to it in his fights before. He doesn’t do much on top but he is definitely a better wrestler. Boser has nothing on his back and is a very bad grappler, so this is a good path to victory. I don’t really know what to think of the odds here, I guess Saricam as fav makes sense but Idk how to feel about it since he’s a nothing burger with no output. Boser ******* sucks though and even with ufc experience/hometown advantage, I don’t think he has some huge advantage. I would not be shocked if this is a razor close fight and Boser wins a robbery though. This seems intriguing on paper but I think this may end up being the worst fight of the night. These two are both awful watches.

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Burns vs Malott
Malott KO RD 1

Jesus this main event sucks, might be the worst one all year this far. Not only is it unappealing from a matchup standpoint, but why do we need a Malott headliner? I get he’s a big name but he’s not some massive draw, nor do they need a Canadian to headline a fight night. The odds on this fight are ridiculous too; I get Burns is shot to pieces but Malott -600 against anyone is insane. He hasn’t looked good since the Magny fight and the one finish he’s had, Malott was looking horrible every time Radtke landed on him. I was confident in Malott against Holland because I thought he had a huge grappling path + Holland is Holland. What proceeded was not only one of the worst fights of 2025, but an embarrassing showing from Malott. Not only did he not use his grappling, he fouled Holland badly to the point it really should’ve been an NC, and he STILL couldn’t do anything more than spar on the feet with him. Tbf, Holland is huge and dangerous (which Burns is not currently), but it was still an awful look. He did not want to be there and was badly compromised yet Malott still fought like a *****. He’s also 34 so its not like he’s some prospect either; you could honestly argue he’s already regressed from the Magny fight lol. He clearly has PTSD from that fight because he’s fought like a complete *****; he doesn’t grapple anymore and on the feet he just sits at range and picks at you with his jab/leg kicks. Malott is a failed industry plant who is now struggling to look good against the layups. The only way he covers -600 here is if he finishes Burns in r1. Now, as badly as I want to fade Malott I just don’t know if I can do it. This is a laughable step down in comp for Burns but he is washed as ****. None of his losses are bad but they are not competitive fights, and he’s also lost every round in these fights (I have no idea how you could score 2 rounds to Burns in the JDM fight). He is washed in every sense of the word, both athletically and physically. He has so much mileage from the wars and weight cuts in his career that it’s just painful to watch. He has no explosions anymore and looks terrible on the feet. Morales is obviously just a demon but seeing Burns get hurt with every shot he landed was just so bad. Burns had that all time war with Chimaev but ever since he lost that dawg in him; he can’t strike or take punches the same way anymore, and as a result his bad flaws are amplified. He has been scared to strike and looked really bad when hit in every fight. It’s crazy to say that this is a competitive fight on the feet, but I feel like I have to back Malott here simply because he isn’t Gilbert Burns. Burns doesn’t have much power and is so slow, he is also going to be very passive which is the type of fight Malott wants on the feet. He still has power so I think he can catch Burns and put him out; Malott may fight like a ***** but Burns cannot take shots anymore, he will go down if he connects flush. I absolutely hate Malott’s striking though, he’s very low volume, cant take shots, is a major gas bag, and scared to actually fight. If he dfoesn’t find a finish early, this could end up being one of the worst fights of all time. Burns does have the grappling edge here but honestly I don’t think that will be very successful either. Malott’s tdd is nonexistent but Burns has never had great control imo. Everyone gets to their feet pretty easily and the JDM fight was the onyl time in recent history that he had a lot of success with wrestling (and we now know JDM had one arm for most of that fight). If this was right after the Brady fight I would be all over Burns at this price, but in 2026 I just don’t know if he has it in him. He is like 50 in fight years and people who lose 14 rounds in a row do not magically get back on that path. I also pray to god this ends in a finish because im genuinely scared how bad this is going to be if this goes all 5 rounds.

Phillips vs Jourdain
Jourdain DEC

I’m glad to see Jourdain back so quickly, I’ve really liked him at 135. He’s still the same fighter in terms of his flaws/gameplan, but this is clearly the weight class for him. He performs much better against guys his size and looks so much stronger here. He’s also gotten in ridiculous shape too; he looks like a new person and has added muscle. I didn’t like that he got taken down and controlled at times against Henry, but in clinch exchanges he was manhandling him. I still think Kyler is a really skilled fighter but it seems like he has peaked. I really thought he’d turned a corner with his cardio but then he started fighting ranked guys again and just got smoked. His natural talent and athleticism is insane, and he’s skilled everywhere but he simply just doesn’t have the cardio to support how he wants to fight. I wasn’t shocked he slowed bad against Lok Dog as that was a terrible style for him, but the way he gassed against Font was terrible. He wanted nothing to do with the striking and gassed himself out after 6 minutes going for takedowns. He didn’t even get that much control either, and was done the second Font got him going on the back foot. This fight is tricky because Phillips definitely is the more skilled fighter, I’m just not sure he’s got the cardio to pull it off. He can definitely outgrapple Jourdain but watching his fights back, Kyler has never been someone who dominates on the mat. His takedowns are great but he doesn’t get much control per takedown, and he will give up positions to go for subs. Jourdain’s tdd is still really bad but at 135 he has the strength to deal with it better. He’s a black belt so I think he can survive a triangle attempt from Kyler (his go-to sub), and he should be able to get to his feet since Kyler presents a lot of opportunities for you to get up. MMA math never works but if Kyler wasn’t able to pull off a wrestling heavy gameplan and sub Font, then I don’t think it will work here. Again, MMA math doesn’t work but both guys have bad tdd, and Font much worse defensive grappling than Jourdain so I think it’s an apt comparison. On the feet, I feel pretty confident in Jourdain winning. Kyler is a super dynamic striker but these last couple fights have shown that all goes away when you put him on the back foot. He’s also just a bad boxer in general; his kicking game is amazing and he does have good punching mechanics, but his overall boxing is not good. He struggles a lot in the pocket and hates any form of pressure. He also relies heavily on his dynamic movement as defense and keeps his hands low, so when pressured he panics and overreacts to avoid strikes; causing him to gas quicker. Jourdain gets hit a lot and needs a brawl to have success, but Kyler’s style will invite that. Even if Kyler is going first he still fights at a high pace which is something Jourdain thrives in. Jourdain is also the bigger guy here, thus making his style even more effective. Like I said earlier, he’s also added on a ton of muscle and looks a career best in terms of strength/physicality. He’s still not as good of an athlete as Kyler but he is strong as hell and still a good athlete himself. I can see Kyler winning r1 with tds and pressure, but I think he is going to be in hell at the halfway mark. Jourdain is going to be able to pressure him and is the superior boxer here. I also just have a really hard time backing Kyler knowing how bad his gas tank is; even if he doesn’t completely gas out he still significantly slows round by round. Jourdain also has momentum on his side that’s hard to deny; he paid his dues at 145 taking hard fights and in the wrong weight class, now he’s leveraging that experience in a new weight class and is in his physical prime. I’ve always been a Kyler fan and his skillset/natural talent is awesome but he clearly has hit his ceiling and has some fatal flaws that are hard to ignore. Jourdain is not perfect and I’m not sure where his ceiling is since he fights the same he always has, but he clearly belongs at 135. I could maybe see a finish here but Kyler is so tough. A Jourdain guillotine is always live, especially since Kyler will be shooting and will get sloppy with his attempts, but I could see an attritional tko on the feet if Jourdain is able to really break him down with pressure.

Nallo vs Herbert
Nallo KO RD 3

I have a feeling this may end up being really boring given these two’s styles but I really like this matchmaking. Nallo is older so he needed a debut at the higher end of the rankings, and Hebert has been a gatekeeper for the better guys at 155 for a while. These two have similar builds and similar styles as well. They’re both low volume strikers who use their length well. I don’t think Nallo has a high ceiling given his age, but I really like his game. Everything is basic but in the best way; he doesn’t do anything flashy and sticks to the fundamentals. He fights behind his jab and mixes in a lot of leg kicks. He has a 100% finish rate in both wins and losses, but when you watch him fight everything is super patient and measured; he either finds a finish or gets finished out of nowhere. He’s honestly your quintessential Tristar guy; a very unassuming dude who should be past his physical prime, yet he’s really skilled and has a very safe, yet technical game. Herbert is a good test for him since he will both strike and fight at that measured pace that Nallo prefers. Herbert is such a weird fighter because he’s really talented and is bigger than some WWs, yet he’s a mental midget who fights to the level of his opponents. Herbert does a lot of nothing and ever since the Ilia KO he’s been extremely passive. He’s a really skilled striker and doesn’t make a ton of mistakes, the caveat is just that he throws nothing and honestly seems kind of disinterested when he’s in the cage. He’s had some close fights with guys much better than him and Nallo, but at the end of the day idk if we can read much into it since Jai tends to fight up/down to the level of his competition. They were also close fights because both were happy to sit at range and do nothing. I kind of have a feeling that’s what this fight will turn out to be, and in that scenario I like Nallo here. Fighting someone like Jai will be interesting since Nallo is used to having a huge size advantage, but the same applies to Jai too; I think Jai will be less phased though since he’s got so much experience. In this low volume striking match at range, I like Nallo’s chances. I think he does more and is a better minute winner; one thing about these Tristar guys is they are all masters of closing out really close fights. Despite the measured pace, Nallo has a ton of power and is really good at finding finishes or having the bigger moments in his fights. Neither guy has great durability but Jai has always had a really bad chin. Nallo’s durability is weird; it’s easy to look at his record and see the 3-3 Bellator record with all losses by finish, but I wouldn’t necessarily say he’s fragile. Much like his wins, Nallo’s finish losses were all measured fights where it was either 1-1 or he was winning, and then just sold out of nowhere; maybe it’s a mental lapse more than durability. His leg gave out from kicks against Mota, he was smoking Brown but ran into a cross and got finished, and then Picolotti just grinded him out with wrestling. The latter shouldn’t matter but the first two could be concerning given that Nallo lost focus and ran into hard shots. His defense is normally very good but like all Tristar guys, he is a perfectionist; meaning he usually stays sharp but the few times he does get hit, it usually is a really big shot. Jai’s experience and reach could contribute to this and cause some issues. Nallo is usually a heavy leg kicker so he should be fine, but Jai does attack the calf a lot himself. Mota was causing some issues with them in their fight, although it didn’t have an effect until late. I think the striking will be really close but I’ve got to marginally favor Nallo here. Jai’s chin will always be a liability to me and in these close fights I think Tristar guys actually end up doing pretty well at stealing the moments. I don’t think we see muhc grappling from either guy here, but Nallo sub could be super interesting. He doesn’t wrestle much but Jai does like to force the clinch in his fights. Nallo has a very good d’arce choke that he uses as a counter when people try grappling, as well as sneaky back takes of his own. Nallo is not good off of his back but when he gets you down he is pretty crafty; he’s a black belt in both JJJ and BJJ too, so he has both disciplines. Jai has always had a really bad ground game and hasn’t been wrestled in a while, so maybe this could be a sneaky path. This is a close fight and it could end in disaster for Nallo, but I actually think he’s kind of good. He won’t go that far due to age but he is a crafty fighter.

posts.content:
[Predictions]

Phillips vs. Jourdain
Charles Jourdain, DEC
I’m tempted to pick Phillips as he’s more dynamic on the feet and has a decent reach advantage here. However I am going to lean Jourdain at the moment. He’s the more technical striker of the two and he also has a grappling advantage. I also think Jourdain can make Phillips fight off the back foot which he does not like at all. Phillips also has some of the worst conditioning in the entire division and rarely ever looks good after the first round. His style could be confusing for Jourdain and it could just throw him off of his game entirely, but if it doesn’t then Jourdain should win a clear 29-28.

Nallo vs. Herbert
Mandel Nallo, TKO, R2
Much like the last fight, I’m also tempted to take the underdog here. He’s a good striker with good grappling defense and has fought way better competition. You can even argue he beat both Klein and Padilla. However at some point I have to stop trusting him. For one he has a pretty bad chin. And he also makes his fights a lot closer than they need to be a lot of the time which has costed him a few times before. And in some fights he’ll be too gunshy to really do anything such as in his fight with Ziam. So while I don’t think Nallo is really that special at all, I really have to pick him here. He’s around the skill level of Jai which is honestly good enough to beat him even if he is a half step below. He is for sure going to land the better shots out of the two and has a lot more finishing potential. And since Jai likes to fight close, that part can very easily come back to bite him if this fight reaches the cards. But I’m actually going to take Nallo by finish. He is actually very good at finishing fights and he definitely has the power to hurt Jai. And you can’t imagine that Jai’s chin is getting any better with age. I want to pick Herbert here but I have to take Nallo.

Jasudavicius vs. Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Great bounce-back fight for Jasudavicius. Both of these girls like to grapple and Jasudavicius is very clearly the better grappler of the two. Silva has good offensive grappling herself, but her defensive grappling leaves a lot to be desired. She has great submissions and decent control, but she isn’t the greatest off of her back and she can be taken down and controlled. I would also say Jasmine is the better finisher of the two. 30-27 Jasudavicius

Moisés vs. Young
Gauge Young, DEC
I’m not sure why everyone’s on Moises. Moises has been exposed plenty of times now for being just pure BJJ with decent leg kicks. His striking overall isn’t great at all and he’s a horrible wrestler. I don’t see him having much success getting Young down, and if he does manage to get him down he doesn’t have good control either. And Young is also a way better striker. I don’t think Young is amazing, but he should be good enough to beat Moises.

Buzukja vs. Barbosa
Márcio Barbosa, TKO, R1
Yeah Barbosa’s winning this one. Buzukja simply does not belong here. Easy money

Valentin vs. Leblanc
Julien Leblanc, TKO, R2
Leblanc sucks and is a complete can crusher so I see why some people on the forum are picking Valentin. And it may even be worth a sprinkle. But at the end of the day those same exact traits apply to Valentin. He’s not good nor has he beaten anyone good either. It’s really just a flip a coin type of fight. Though I am going to pick Leblanc because to me Valentin is an auto fade.

Boser vs. Saricam
Gokhan Saricam, DEC
What an absolute joke of a fight this is. Not even going to say anything. Saricam probably wins. Absolute stinker

Croden vs. Zheleznyakova
Melissa Croden, DEC
Similar styles. Leaning Croden though because she seems to take it a bit more serious I think. Darya looks like she cares more about her appearance with her botox. Plus if this is close, we’re in Canada and Croden is Canadian. And most WMMA fights are close.

Aldrich vs. Horth
Jamey-Lyn Horth, DEC
🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮🤮

Raposo vs. Nascimento
Allan Nascimento, SUB, R1
Raposo is the definition of a nothing burger. He has a little bit of skill everywhere, but does absolutely nothing. Against Lima he stood right in front of him for three rounds while Lima was kicking the **** out of his legs and almost got gifted a decision. And then almost got gifted a decision again when he fought Su. In a fight where he once again just didn’t impress. And then he beat Maksum, but Maksum realistically didn’t even have a legitimate win in the UFC. The Nam fight was a robbery and then he lost to everyone else so beating him isn’t exactly an amazing achievement. He’s someone who also does nothing at times. Nascimento should be able to outgrapple Mitch and eventually find a submission. I really don’t believe that Raposo is a UFC level guy at all and I hope he gets cut after.

Castañeda vs. Vologdin
John Castañeda, DEC
Castaneda isn’t amazing, but I’m going to take him here. He’s shown that he can compete with UFC level guys despite not being a very high level UFC guy. And he isn’t horrible by any means. He’s a bit of a jack of all trades kind of fighter who just has some skills everywhere to compete with his opponent wherever the fight goes. Vologdin just flat out doesn’t cut it for me. He’s shown great finishing ability, but it’s against cans. And then when he finally fought a half decent guy on DWCS he lost. He’s clearly nowhere near this level at all and hopefully he doesn’t stay here for too long. I’ll gladly take Casteneda at plus odds. Especially considering he’s 3 inches taller and has a 6 inch reach advantage.

Siraj vs. Yannis
Jamie Siraj, SUB, R2
Easy debut for Siraj. Yannis is a complete jobber who does not belong here and cannot grapple to save his life. Fits right into Siraj’s game. But this will probably be the only fight in the UFC that Siraj has where he actually looks good. I don’t think he’s UFC level either. He’s just a level above Yannis.

posts.author: Fast_Tulip
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Burns vs. Malott
Gilbert Burns, SUB, R4
This might be a really stupid pick and I might change it before it locks in, but right now I'll take Burns. Malott has not been impressive so far. He does have a lot of finishes so I will give him credit there, but he got hit by a decent amount of clean shots in the Holland fight (thought he lost that), and can't forget about him gassing out against Magny after grappling for a bit. I don't think Burns really has the power anymore to hurt Mallott, but I think he might be able to land a few clean shots to get some respect and blast some double leg takedowns. Will Mallott gas after wrestling for a few rounds? Who knows, but I can't really see Malott finishing him. He's definitely not subbing him, and Burns chin really isn't that bad. His loss streak isn't bad when you actually look into it. He was fighting Belal with one arm, was close to winning the Jack fight before he got hit with the knee, went to a decision with one of the best grapplers in the division, and got KOed by one of the harder punchers in the division. Getting outstruck by Brady isn't actually that bad of a look. Brady looked pretty good against Burns, and the Morales fight would have been more competitive for Brady if he wasn't so chinny. Anyways, I think Burns manages to grapple Mallott for a few rounds, gasses him out, and finds a sub in the late 4th or 5th rounds. Burns cardio didn't look that bad against Brady as he was able to pick him up and throw him down in the 4th round. GIVE ME BURNS!!!!

Edit: I rewatched the Holland fight again and Malott won R2, but Holland was still hurting downstairs bad after R1

Burns was on his way to building a nice streak again until he got hurt early in his fight with Belal. He then almost rebounded and was gonna beat JDM on the scorecards until he got caught, which to be fair happened because he was gassing hard.

posts.author: Drew Dobers Chin
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Anyone else jumping on the Kyler Phillips train this fight? I know he lost his last two, but they were against two solid top 15 135 contenders. Lokdog weight bullied him the whole fight, and Rob Font is a really good, crafty vet.
Up until those fights, Phillips was for the most part "undefeated" in the UFC. The reason for the quotes is his majority decision loss to Paiva, which should have been scored a draw (Phillips 10-8 rd1, Paiva 10-9 rds 2 & 3).
Meanwhile, if we shift over to Charles Jourdain's record, we can see his last two wins are submission finishes, which look really good on paper until you take a closer look and realize he's being fed unc cans to bring his record back up. Anytime Jourdain has faced competition on his level, he has never seemed able to perform. For example, his loss to Jean Silva showed he often tries to be too technical and takedown dependent, while his fight with Sean Woodson showed what happens when Jourdain can't get those takedowns in (0/4 Landed).
I think this should be a classic Matrix performance from Kyler Phillips and a fight of the night for sure. The striking favors Phillips, and he has amazing TDD, as shown in the Raoni Barcelos fight (8/11 Defended). I even saw odds-wise that the +3.5 spread for Phillips was the same as the moneyline for Jourdain. I'm currently eyeing up Phillips & over 1.5 rounds at +155 for a really solid parlay piece. Does anyone agree or disagree? If so, I'd be interested to hear why.

posts.content:
[Predictions]

@Josh7 we could consider Mitch Raposo to be on a 7-fight win streak... but should we?

In Raposo's fight against Lima, two judges scored it 30-27 for Lima, as did every media member. I can only really vaguely remember that fight, but Lima out-struck Raposo every round. Fun fact: the same judge (Dave Tirelli) gave Paulo Costa a 49-46 scorecard against Sean Strickland on the same night, when the other two gave it to Strickland 50-45 and 49-46.

In Raposo's fight against Sumudaerji, two judges scored it 29-28 for Sumudaerji, and the majority of media members scored it the same or 30-27. The judge who scored it 29-28 for Raposo was Junichiro Kamijo, who gave round 2 to Raposo despite him landing a total of 2 strikes, to Sumudaerji's 17. Raposo landed 1 of 6 takedowns that round, for 76 seconds of control time. I don't remember this fight at all though. Still, fun facts: the same judge thought that Lance Gibson Jr beat Bobby Green, that Cody Brundage beat Donte Johnson, and that Ciryl Gane beat Alexander Volkov.

In conclusion? MMA judges are terrible and probably corrupt. And I think Nascimento chokes out Raposo once Raposo engages in the wrestling.

If Burns gets it to the ground early, I like his chances.
If it stays on the feet, Malott probably has the advantage.

I’m leaning Malott, but wouldn’t be surprised if Burns controls him on the mat.

posts.author: Psykhes
posts.content:
[Predictions]

The first fight of the card seems a lot trickier of a pick to me than the odds, and especially the % of picks on Tap, would suggest. All we’ve seen of Yannis in the UFC is him getting run over up a weight class in a fight he never had a chance in, his regional tape doesn’t look any worse than Siraj’s. I’ve only watched two Siraj fights so far but he seems incredibly grappling dependent and his takedown entries are really poor, he goes flat to his knees and gets zero drive so it’s always just an entry into a high crotch or a clinch position and he has to work again for the actual takedown from there, and he doesn’t seem to be strong enough to win those exchanges consistently either. Gustavo Erak was winning most of that fight in my eyes and seemed the notably stronger man, which would be excusable if Siraj wasn’t also out-muscled by Brandau the fight prior, Siraj did find a slick way to scramble to Erak’s back and locked in the choke impressively quickly, but he was having a very hard time up til that moment and he used the same move to scramble up from bottom over and over, that could easily say as much about Erak’s deficiencies as Siraj’s strengths. And he goes for the same toothless takedowns against an ancient Diego Brandau too, can’t get them and gets absolutely dusted. Brandau even reverses him and gets his own takedown from one of the clinches Siraj is so desperately forcing, and picks him up and power slams him out of another, that’s a 37 yr old man basically ragdolling him the same way Erak did.  Siraj just seems so bjj dependent and so willing to play from guard and roll for dumb leg locks, I don’t love him as a big favorite here even though Yannis looked so bad in his debut. Kind of feels like we’re supposed to see Jannis, go o ya that guy sucks, see he’s up against a Canadian “prospect,” and take for granted he’s being fed to the wolves here but I’m not sure that’s the case. Kind of like with LeBlanc and Valentin only people seem to have started to look past Valentin’s losing streak to the fact that LeBlanc isn’t very good so the odds have flipped for that one

On a recent evening, Pasha Boyer, a thirty-two-year-old father and amateur sports bettor who lives in Missoula, Montana, logged on to TikTok to continue what has become an unpaid full-time job: debating Israel vs. Palestine over TikTok Live. As usual, his screen resembled a nightmarish Zoom call, with the seven other interlocutors locked in a perpetual state of disagreement. “They said literally everything I was saying was wrong,” he told me. The acrimony did not deter him. He stayed on hour after hour, as fourteen hundred viewers came and went, and he eventually settled into a debate with a fellow-Jew who disagreed with Boyer’s pro-Israel stance. By the time Boyer logged off, it was 3 p.m. the next day; eighteen hours had elapsed. “You know what the crazy part is?” he said. “The other guy was on until three o’clock in the afternoon as well.”

With TikTok’s cultural influence well established, many of its users are now exploring whether the platform can host constructive debates. Boyer is one of several dozen people who have pushed this question to its limit, hashing out one of the world’s most contentious and intractable issues on TikTok Live, the app’s most freewheeling feature. Whether Boyer and his fellow-debaters can change minds is unclear. But, given the opportunity to engage with real people in front of large audiences, staunchly pro-Israel and pro-Palestine users have decided to join live streams that allow them to express their views—and argue with others—seemingly forever.

[Predictions]

Burns vs. Malott
Mike Malott, TKO, R2
Malott -600???. Insane even for an ultra washed 40 year-old 0-4 Burns in Canada. Burns' losses have aged well and this is a step-down but does it matter when he’s losing every round in all of them? And you can literally see his decline. Physically and mentally. He’s been through a lot of damage and weightcuts, He looks delayed and slow on the feet, He doesn’t have much power, He cannot take a shot, And he just doesn’t fight like that dog he was before. His skill on the ground is still there but he’s not gonna control Malott for 5 rounds. All of this makes me feel like I have to pick Malott. But he’s nothing special either, that's the problem. A 34 year-old Canadian prospect who’s actually scared to fight. He’s been horrified ever since he fumbled the ball to Magny. Since then it’s been Trevin Giles, Shaky Radtke performance, And he barely beat Kevin Holland with an illegal shot. As much as I want to fade Malott finally I doubt he fumbles this, He somewhat has power. He can clip 40 year-old Gilbert Burns early.

Phillips vs. Jourdain
Charles Jourdain, DEC
I think Jourdain has looked good at 135, but he’s a little overrated. Kyler is a good fighter who looks great in round one, but has extreme cardio issues. Unless Kyler has completely fixed himself I can see Jourdain taking over. Jourdain has fumbled plenty of times though and kyler is a real fighter and has the better comp on paper. Jourdain is probably the more technical striker, He has a grappling advantage, Cardio advantage, Hometown advantage. Jourdain just has to get past round one and take over with volume if Kyler fades, and don’t get wrestle ******. It’s been done before. Kyler is still very live as a dog, this is a tricky match-up. Unless Jourdain pulls something crazy out of his toolbag I doubt there’s a finish. 3x 29-28 Jourdain. Might switch.

Nallo vs. Herbert
Mandel Nallo, TKO, R2
I wanna take the underdog so bad. I like Jai. He's a good NPC fighter Good crafty striking, Good ground defense, Who has the experience with better competition. The issue is he fights to his competition level and he has a suspicious chin. Nallo isn’t a huge dropoff in the level of fighter, and he has KO power. He’s never seen a decision. Nallo had a good DWCS performance as the underdog he got a KO against a pretty durable opponent using leg kicks and just put him down simply with a straight right. It’s obvious Nallo knows how to finish a fight and Jai has a bad chin and is due for a KO. Jai could get crafty but I think Nallo finds the shot.

Jasudavicius vs. Silva
Jasmine Jasudavicius, DEC
Good match-making for the division. Good bounce back fight for Jasmine. She got embarrassed by Fiorot and her iced out Labubu, but Fiorot’s a monster so I'll let it slide. Karine’s coming off competitive fight with Maycee Barber where she didn’t look bad her offensive grappling is there but her defense kinda let her down in the ground exchanges. Both these fighters are primarily grapplers. And I think Jasmine is the better of the two. Neither are much on their feet. Karine doesn’t do great off her back, and Jasmine is stronger and a better wrestler. She can definitely control Karine. I don’t think Karine’s finishing ability is there vs. high comp. Jasmine probably has the better chance to finish since she’s active on top. 3x 30-27 Jasudavicius.

Moisés vs. Young
Gauge Young, DEC
Should be a very competitive fight that could go either way, weird match-making though. Young has the edge on the feet and could hurt Moises, his pace and aggression should make a difference since Moises doesn’t bring much striking outside of leg kicks and his reactions aren’t great. Young’s wrestling is solid too, so it’s hard to see Moises getting him down easily and controlling him. Moises still has the BJJ, IQ, and experience, but he looked bad against Jared Gordon and has seemed a bit checked out lately, which makes him tough to trust. Gordon’s a good veteran though, it makes you wonder if Young is even good enough or just in a favorable matchup stylistically.

Buzukja vs. Barbosa
Márcio Barbosa, TKO, R1
Should be an easy fight for Barbosa. Funny enough Barbosa is another regional Gabriel Santos victim, how good is Gabriel Santos? Buzujka is a bit of a jobber and not really good at anything, 1-3 in the UFC against decent comp in his losses. He’s gonna fight the exact way Barbosa wants. Buzujka doesn’t have much power to catch Barbosa and he’s hittable. Barbosa is a good striker, very heavy hitter and is a round one or nothing fighter. He should be fun in 145. I don’t see this getting out the first round.

Valentin vs. Leblanc
Robert Valentin, SUB, R2
Regional MMA fight. Never would I think I’d pick Valentin to ever win a UFC fight. But here we are. Leblanc is just awful. Yes Valentin has looked awful in the UFC, but he fought a monster in Ateba, and a complete mismatch in FInney and Loder. Now he gets to fight a middle aged Canadian regional can. Valentin’s TDD is abysmal but he has grappling going for him at least. He should definitely have the advantage on the mat. Leblanc probably has the slight edge in striking but he’s still not good and Valentin can make it competitive. Not like I watched every Leblanc fight but in the one’s I did I saw that he goes into the clinch a lot. I think that’s better for Valentin than him. Maybe Leblanc catches him, but I think Valentin saves his job. He’s more experienced and better on the mat. I think he finds the sub. We'll see how it goes in Canada.

Boser vs. Saricam
Gokhan Saricam, DEC
Another regional MMA fight, this is crazy match-making, this could be the worst fight ever. Both dudes do absolutely nothing in the octagon; they just circle around the entire fight with no output. Both dudes can’t strike or grapple. It’s gonna be a slow low-level sparring match. But one’s dudes coming off a decent Bellator run and one is coming off a 2 and half near layoff. Boser sucks completely and has lost fights simply because his TDD is so bad. I expect a blob fest where Saricam wrestles Boser, probably does slightly more on the feet and wins an awful unanimous decision. 3x 29-28 Saricam.

Croden vs. Zheleznyakova
Melissa Croden, DEC
This is a decent WMMA fight. Both these girls come to fight, are mostly strikers, both get hit a lot. I’m lowk a part of the Croden army. I’ve picked her in her last two and I kinda just wanna keep it going. I like her activity. She’s a good striker, she has a base of wrestling at least, big and physical for the division, and she walks forward and throws down. Zheleznyakova is a bit of the same, without any mat skills. A good striker that throws hard. The tricky thing is Croden uses her size, and Zheleznyakova is the same height and build. It’ll still be competitive, neither woman wears damage well at all. If Croden wrestled a little more then she did I would have an easier time picking her. Croden via split decision. 50/50 fight. Might switch.

Aldrich vs. Horth
Jamey-Lyn Horth, DEC
WMMA fight. Both mediocre. Horth is coming off a quick and impressive KO. Aldrich is coming off a boring decision with a 13-11 fighter. Horth’s physicality and power, along with the fight being in Canada is enough for me to pick her here. Aldrich can’t hurt Horth and can’t wrestle her enough for it to be useful. Probably a really boring fight unless Horth somehow finds a second KO in a row but I doubt it. 3x 29-28 Horth.

Castañeda vs. Vologdin
John Castañeda, DEC
This is one of the bright-spots on this dreadful card. This will be a banger. Castaneda isn’t a world-beater, but he can compete in the UFC, is well rounded, and pushes a great pace. He has decent wins in Miles Johns and Gufarov, and performed well in his losses. Vologdin had an amazing fight on the contender series where I actually picked him, and while it was a great fight he was on the losing end in his first fight against real comp. Also Vologdin is way too small for Bantamweight, he’s shown great finishing ability but I don’t know if he’ll have it at 135 in the UFC. But he’s very tough, he has mad grit, he has decent striking with body work, crazy pressure with big shots. If he’s moving forward in this fight and he can defend the wrestling he has a real chance to win. If he loses he’s still 25 I’d like to see him at 125 with the experience of Castaneda and his power maximized. 3x 29-28 Castaneda. Might switch.

Siraj vs. Yannis
Jamie Siraj, SUB, R2
Yes this fight totally needed to be added of course. I don't think Siraj is amazing, he starts slow but the blueprint to beat Yannis is out there. Get him down, dudes clueless on the mat. Siraj’s offensive wrestling isn’t anything great, but he can find the back and get submissions, which is all he really needs here. Yannis has better stand-up than Siraj but I don’t think it will be there long enough for it to make a huge impact. Siraj is coming down from 145 so he’ll have a size advantage too. Siraj plays it smart in his debut in Canada and gets the submission.
